title: Feasibility of international sources (UK DWI, EU) with license verification
context: The current project scope is US-focused, using EPA/SDWIS data. International expansion to UK (DWI), EU (Drinking Water Directive), and other jurisdictions would broaden the project's public benefit. However, international data sources require per-source license verification before use — the same CI license gate applies. This feasibility study must assess whether open/PD/CC-compatible international sources exist, what their terms permit, and what schema/normalization changes international data would require.
objective: Research the feasibility of adding international drinking-water data sources (UK Drinking Water Inspectorate, EU Drinking Water Directive, and others): (1) identify candidate open/PD/CC-licensed sources; (2) verify each candidate's license terms and confirm or rule out compatibility with the project's license gate; (3) assess schema/normalization changes required for non-US regulatory frameworks (different MCL equivalents, violation categories, unit conventions); (4) identify the regulatory-authority equivalents for 'where to get the official advisory' routing; (5) document findings and a go/no-go recommendation per source.
acceptanceCriteria: Candidate international sources identified (UK DWI, EU DWD, others) with their data URLs and access methods
License terms verified for each candidate; compatibility with the project license gate documented
Schema/normalization changes required for non-US regulatory frameworks assessed
Regulatory-authority equivalents for 'where to get the official advisory' routing identified per jurisdiction
Go/no-go recommendation per source with rationale documented
output: Document: international source feasibility report with license assessments, schema impact analysis, and go/no-go recommendation per source.
